summary: Full backend and frontend testing of Tischplanung app. All 15 backend tests passed (100%). All major UI flows verified: login, registration, event creation, guest management, seating plan, export page, event settings edit, and logout.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal backdrop (fixed inset-0) intercepts pointer events on the event-name input - direct .click() on input fails; workaround required using JS evaluate or force=True + fill

action_items: Fix create-event-modal: The fixed backdrop div intercepts pointer events on the event-name input. Consider using pointer-events-none on the backdrop or restructuring the modal so the input is not a child of the backdrop div. Root cause: `<div class='fixed inset-0 bg-black/40 flex items-center justify-center z-50 p-4'>` intercepts mouse events before they reach the input.
critical_code_review_comments: Modal backdrop pointer-event interception: The fixed backdrop covers the whole screen including the modal content, causing click interception on form inputs inside the modal. This can prevent real users from typing in the event name on some browsers/devices.
server.py is 330 lines - within acceptable range, no split needed
seed_data_creation: Created test user 'uitestuser' (password: test123) and event 'UI Test Event' with guest 'Hans Mueller' during UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend 100% passing. UI mostly working. Test user: uitestuser/test123. Event ID 69bc2095c6e831b650b49f74 was created during testing. Only minor issue: modal backdrop may intercept clicks on event-name-input. All pages (login, dashboard, guests, tischplan, export) load and function correctly. Drag-and-drop not automated tested (DnD requires complex pointer events). Export page shows SVG table visualization with Drucken and PDF herunterladen buttons.
rca_of_the_issue: Modal backdrop div uses 'fixed inset-0' covering entire viewport. When the modal content (input) is a child of this overlay div, Playwright's click mechanism detects the overlay as the interceptor. Fix: Add stopPropagation on modal container click, or use pointer-events-none on backdrop and a separate z-index for content.
